title: OXFORD AL POLICE PARTOL STAFF ROSTER REQUEST AS OF APRIL22,2026.

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Alabama Open Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

Date: April 22, 2026

Good afternoon, I hope this email finds you well and safe.

Pursuant to the Alabama Public Records Law (Ala. Code § 36‑12‑40), I respectfully request a current roster of sworn personnel assigned to standard patrol operations within the Oxford Police Department, including all patrol districts, beats, and field operations units, but excluding special tactical teams and supervisory ranks, as of April 22, 2026.

For each officer, please include:
- Full name
- Badge number or identifier
- Rank or title
- Patrol district, beat, or assignment area
- Unit assignment
- Shift assignment
- Hire date
- K‑9 designation (if applicable)

This request is made for non‑commercial, educational, and research purposes. I am not seeking any confidential personal identifiers or information protected by law. As I am unable to receive mailed records, I kindly request that all responsive documents be emailed to me.

Thank you for your time and consideration.
I hope to hear from you soon.

Sincerely,
Cameron Oliver
Jacksonville, FL
Email: oliverkidsjax@gmail.com
Phone: 904‑489‑9118

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

André Natta, a citizen of Alabama, in coordination with Cameron John Oliver.

title: Newport News Va  Police partol  staff roster request as of April22,2026

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Virginia Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Date: April 22, 2026

Good afternoon, I hope this email finds you well and safe.

Pursuant to the Virginia Freedom of Information Act (Va. Code § 2.2‑3700 et seq.), I respectfully request a current roster of sworn personnel assigned to standard patrol operations within the Newport News Police Department, including all patrol precincts, beats, and field operations units, but excluding special tactical teams and supervisory ranks, as of April 22, 2026.

For each officer, please include:
- Full name
- Badge number or identifier
- Rank or title
- Patrol precinct, beat, or assignment area
- Unit assignment
- Shift assignment
- Hire date
- K‑9 designation (if applicable)

This request is made for non‑commercial, educational, and research purposes. I am not seeking any confidential personal identifiers or information protected by law. As I am unable to receive mailed records, please email all responsive documents.

Thank you for your time and consideration.
I hope to hear from you soon.

Sincerely,
Cameron Oliver
Jacksonville, FL
Email: oliverkidsjax@gmail.com
Phone: 904‑489‑9118

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Tom Nash, a citizen of Virginia, in coordination with Cameron John Oliver.

To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Hello,

Your department has access to ALPRs owned or leased by businesses.

I’m seeking the any lists, contracts, MOUs, and data sharing records that outlines which businesses and properties your agency has access. Examples include: Lowes, Home Depots, malls, HOAs, business improvement districts. 

This information may also be found in Flock Safety or other ALPR company emails that alert the department that a business or private entity is sharing their cameras with you. 

Please search 2025 to today’s date

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Joey Scott
